sentry._note: OAuth — no API key needed. Authenticate via browser on first use.

wiki.description: Query personal LLM wiki — graph-aware synthesis via local LightRAG (no API cost)
wiki.template: Query ~/repos/llm-wiki for: {{input}}. Use the wiki_query tool (wiki-rag MCP) for graph-aware synthesis — it runs locally via ollama, no API cost. For quick exact-term lookups, use qmd as fallback. Cite results as [[concepts/...]] or [[summaries/...]].

brainstorm.description: Brainstorm approaches before implementing — hard gate: no code until design approved
brainstorm.template: Brainstorm 3-5 concrete approaches for: {{input}}. For each: name, how it works, trade-offs (speed/simplicity/maintainability/risk). No code yet. Pick the best approach and explain why. Wait for approval before implementing.

tdd.description: Test-driven development: write failing test first, then implementation
tdd.template: Apply TDD for: {{input}}. Step 1: Write a failing test that captures the requirement. Step 2: Show the minimal implementation to make it pass. Step 3: Refactor if needed. Use red-green-refactor discipline.

debug.template: Systematic debug for: {{input}}. Step 1: Reproduce the issue with minimal case. Step 2: Form 2-3 hypotheses about root cause. Step 3: Test each hypothesis. Step 4: Fix the confirmed root cause. Do NOT jump to fixes before diagnosis.

security.description: Security audit using OWASP Top 10 + AI-specific checks
security.template: Security audit of: {{input}}. Walk OWASP Top 10 systematically. Also check: indirect prompt injection (if agent code), agentic sandbox controls, secret exposure. Report findings as [SEVERITY] Category — Description / Location / Fix.

review.description: Code review: correctness, security, performance, maintainability
review.template: Review this code: {{input}}. Check: correctness (logic errors, edge cases), security (OWASP top issues), performance (obvious bottlenecks), maintainability (readability, naming, patterns). Report issues with file:line references. Flag severity: critical/high/medium/low.

plan.description: Write an implementation plan before coding
plan.template: Write an implementation plan for: {{input}}. Include: goal, approach, files to create/modify (with why), step sequence, risks/unknowns. No code yet. Present plan for approval.

verify.description: Verification checklist before claiming work is done
verify.template: Verify completion of: {{input}}. Run through: 1) Does it match the original requirement? 2) Edge cases handled? 3) Tests pass? 4) No regressions in related areas? 5) Code readable and correctly named? Report pass/fail for each.

coder.description: Full-stack code implementation (maps to: Claude Code code-writer). Use for implementing features from clear requirements, refactoring, prototyping. Invoke when requirements are defined. Future: migrate to opencode/gpt-5.3-codex once OpenCode billing is active.

reviewer.description: Code review specialist (maps to: Claude Code code-reviewer). Reviews for correctness, security, performance, maintainability. Sonnet-tier: sufficient for routine review; use architect for architectural assessment.

debugger.description: Debugging specialist (maps to: Claude Code backend-debug-tester). Finds, replicates, fixes bugs. Diagnoses root cause before fixing. Use when a bug is reported or tests fail.

architect.description: Architecture and design reviewer (maps to: Claude Code architecture-reviewer). Opus-tier for structural assessment, scalability, hidden risk. Invoke before major implementation.

security-auditor.description: Security audit specialist (maps to: Claude Code security-auditor). OWASP-depth analysis, secrets scanning, indirect prompt injection checks. Use for pre-deploy audits or security-critical changes.

fast-coder.description: Fast boilerplate and routine code generation (maps to: Claude Code code-writer-fast). Haiku-tier: scaffolding, simple utilities, repetitive patterns only. Not for complex features.
